result: F-HOLO-7 (NEGATIVE/informative): XOR-of-regional-roughness defeats every fixed GLOBAL closed-form (all ~0.52) AND a strong generic MLP (0.661) but is solved by a fixed REGIONAL closed-form (oracle 0.935). => bias-discriminating, NOT strictly learning-necessary. Gate 2 (strict) NOT met.

novelty_classification.note: Guards against scaling on an unmet gate; reframes the next test as bias-discrimination (holonomy/entropy model vs generic MLP) rather than learning-necessity.

guards_added: Gate-2 verdict: do not claim learning-necessary or scale (concentric/SLAM) — a fixed regional closed-form solves it

source_changes: src/curvature_field.rs (realize_field refactor behavior-preserving + sample_regional_curvature + region_roughness_diff + 2 tests)

gates.gate2_strict: FAIL (informative): fixed-global all chance, MLP 0.661 (insufficient), oracle 0.935 is a FIXED regional closed-form => not learning-necessary
gates.tests: 168 passed / 0 failed (2 new; F-HOLO-4 tests still pass after refactor)

integrity_note: The MLP was given a fair strong attempt (128 hidden, 600 train, 700 ep; 0.542->0.661) to rule out undertraining before concluding 'generic learner insufficient'. Not forced to a pass.
honest_scope: Known-region-split only; a strictly-learning-necessary (latent partition) task is deferred as genuinely harder. SLAM/concentric remain gated.
next_experiment_authorized: BIAS-DISCRIMINATION test: the holonomy/entropy model (global_entropy_pool + cpml_tier regional pooling) vs the generic MLP on THIS task — does the framework bias close the 0.66->0.94 gap? Control with a plain conv/pooling net to separate 'holonomy-specific' from 'any 2nd-order bias'.
next_experiment_proposal: build a small regional-pooling readout (per-region global_entropy_pool -> 2 features -> tiny MLP) and A/B vs the generic MLP and a conv baseline on curvature_xor_gate2; if the regional-pooling model reaches ~oracle and the generic MLP/conv do not, the framework's regional 2nd-order bias is the lever (a meaningful, honest capability claim short of 'learning necessary').
